programmes.show_name: In Their Own Words: Little Simz
programmes.show_description: 6/6. Highlights of Little Simz's appearances on the BBC, including interviews and sessions.
2/6. Another chance to hear Little Simz from when she took over the airwaves as part of 6 Music's Wise Women Season. Expect cuts from Childish Gambino, Kelis and Fela Kuti.
3/6. Little Simz's best live performances from the BBC Archive as part of her Artist Collection. Including early Radio 1 sessions from 2014 and her appearance at the 6Music Festival 2019.
3/6. A playlist celebrating the music of Little Simz and its roots and branches - including those they've been influenced by and those they've inspired.
The biggest tunes to start the day, plus there's your chance to vote on a Lost Love.
Wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Nemone sits in with the finest blend of fantastic music for your Tuesday morning.
Craig celebrates one of the greatest albums of all time: Thin Lizzy's 'Jailbreak'... With Thin Lizzy guitarist Scott Gorham, artist Jim Fitzpatrick, and Irish band SPRINTS.
Simon Raymonde and Abbey Raymonde join Huw as he turns the spotlight on one of the UK's most influential independent record labels, Bella Union.
Brighton band Lime Garden are live in session from Maida Vale., following the release of their latest single All Bad Parts.
Gideon Coe plays his music loves from every era and genre.
2/4. Tonight's music captures the feeling of when Jacob was a teenager going to the North of Scotland and staying in their best friend's shack, in the town of Rannoch.
Nathan celebrates the best indie of the 2010s, with music from Tame Impala and HAIM.
Another chance to hear Mark Lamarr in full swing from September 2009... this time with The Heavy in session.
1/4. 6 Music Festival 2026 headliner Courtney Barnett shares her songwriting inspirations.
Want to hear the latest tracks 6 Music have added to their playlist? Then listen here!
Names in Songs returns, can you hear your name in a well-known song?
Wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Film-makers Iain Forsyth and Jane Pollard join to talk about their new documentary film, Broken English, which is an exploration of the life and career of Marianne Faithfull.
Craig celebrates your midweek wins for Wednesday, I'm In Love, and we hear another of your left-field music suggestions as we turn Left At The Lights.
New discoveries from Huw and we get to know Ellen Beth Abdi in this week's 'Huw Are You'.
Witch Post chat to Nathan about the making of their new EP Butterfly, which is out on 20th March. The Scottish-American duo were formerly session guests and our Breaking Artist.
Marc Riley and Gideon Coe come together to play their music loves from every era and genre.
3/4. Tonight is all about Jacob's favourite queer artists that inspire them, and make them feel more possible, with a folk lean.
